observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC), or other officer assigned, as the case may be, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will submit the required documents.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination between offices, but commit to comply with the requirements.

observations.recommendation:
1.6. 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor assured to comply the following measures:
a. 	Require the Human Resource Management Office to provide adequate manpower and resources to conduct a complete physical count of PPE.
b. 	Require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor on or before January 31 of the following year.
c. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Review and reclassify PPE items below ₱50,000 as semi-expendable property in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004. Adjustments should be made to ensure accurate financial reporting; and
Keep PPELC updated and complete.
d. 	Require GSO to keep PPEPC likewise updated and complete, and to conduct regular reconciliation with the Accounting Office for accurate property records.
e. 	Implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts procedures as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish verifiable PPE balances as to existence, condition, and accountability.

3.6. 	We recommended and the management assured compliance to the following:
a. Inventory Committee to submit a Report on the Physical Count of Local Roads (RPCLR) is submitted to the audit team on or before January 31 of each year.
b. 	General Service Office – Designate to:
Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card to reflect and complete the data on local road assets.
Prepare and submit the Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) to the audit team on or before January 31 of each year.
c. 	 Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain a complete Local Road Network Ledger Card reflecting the complete data on local road accounts.
Report the total value of the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements using the required pro forma disclosure.

5.7. 	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant assured to exert maximum effort in tracing the unidentified balances and effect necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account balance in the financial statements. Moreover, it is recommended that adequate disclosure pertaining to the prior period adjustment be provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

6.7. 	We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following measures:
a. Create a General Services Office (GSO) position, rather than just designating one, to oversee inventory management, ensure proper recording of procured goods, and coordinate with concerned offices.
b. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to perform the following:
To use the correct entry in setting up inventory for procured items and supplies as prescribed in the NGAS Manual;
Stop the practice of treating the procured inventory items/goods as outright expense to prevent misstatement of both the inventory and expense accounts and at the same time, strengthen the inventory control and accountability mechanism.
c. 	Require the General Services Office (GSO) or the designated property custodian to properly record, monitor, and maintain inventory records for procured items, ensuring that issuances are supported by appropriate documents such as Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Material Issued.

8.5. 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor assured to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008 by maintaining a Subsidiary Ledger (SL) for GAD with an assigned SL code to properly track all GAD-related expenses, programs, activities, and projects (PAPs).

9.13. 	We recommended the Local Chief Executive as the Chairperson of the Local School Board, to ensure compliance with the provisions of Section 6.1 of the JMC are properly carried out by the agency officials and employees concerned and to instruct:
a. The LSB Secretary to ensure that copies of the SEF Budget furnished to the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are properly transmitted and duly stamped “RECEIVED”, signed and dated by the receiving officer of the office concerned; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd, copy furnished the local Sangguniang concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

10.9. 	We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Submit the Report of Check Issued (RCI), Report of Disbursement (RD), and Liquidation Report (LR) together with the required supporting documents on the disbursements of funds; and the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) together with the Duplicate Copies of the Accountable Forms issued and deposit slips weekly or within 5 days at the end of the month to the Municipal Accounting Office.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents; and
Keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

11.9. 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through the BAC Secretariat to comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and pertinent provisions of the IRR of RA 9184. Also, submit the required supporting documents by complying with the contract review checklist to facilitate the review thereof.

12.6. 	We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008 by maintaining a Subsidiary Ledger (SL) for GAD with an assigned SL code to properly track all GAD-related expenses, programs, activities, and projects (PAPs).

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee establish and adopt appropriate measures and controls on the disbursements of funds, not to solely rely on the availability of appropriations/allotments but the cash availability as well. Emergency austerity measures should also be established to cover up existing cash overdrafts at the earliest possible time.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Check Issued (RCI), Report of Disbursement (RD), and Liquidation Report (LR) together with the required supporting documents on the disbursements of funds; and Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) together with the Duplicate Copies of the Accountable Forms issued and deposit slips weekly or within 5 days at the end of the month.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit the SEF Annual Budget and the corresponding Utilization Reports and lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents;

b. Ensure the submission of DVs segregated by fund; and

c. Keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Budget Officer as Head of the Budget Unit to submit the monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for both the current and continuing appropriations to the Municipal Accountant and the latter to submit the SAOOB together with the monthly trial balance to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following measures:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and submit the complete RPCPPE for all types of property of the LGU;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and reconcile their property records; and
c. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish verifiable PPE balances as to existence, condition and accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a) Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit an Agency Inspection Report for 100% and the Certificate of Final Acceptance to form part of the documentary requisites for payment of final billing. Also, submit the said report and certificate together with other necessary documents supporting to letter request for COA Technical Inspection;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the documentary requirements under Section 9.1.1.4. of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payment of final billing and retention money shall be made; and
c) Strictly impose the mandatory posting of warranty security for infrastructure projects to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3. of the IRR of R.A 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 COA Circular No.2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Manpower constraints and laxity of Management to comply with the documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Manpower constraints and laxity of Management to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.reason: This particular audit recommendation was not complied with yet due to workload or man-power constraints in the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC comply with the pertinent provisions under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 02-2020, particularly on the preparation of the APP and changes made thereto, the PMR to include all procurement activities undertaken through competitive bidding and other alternative modes of procurement and the APCPI Results. Henceforth, electronically submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Further, copies of these reports be furnished to the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as the basis for the auditor’s action, if any.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations by the Audit Team were still not implemented due to manpower and time constraints.
observations.reason: The recommendations by the Audit Team were still not implemented due to manpower and time constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require inspection of all the deliveries of goods, equipment, services and completed/accomplished infrastructure projects by the Agency Inspection Committee or the Agency Inspector and the COA representative, and deliveries are accepted and approved by the Agency Head. Further, it is recommended that claims of Suppliers/Contractors only be paid when supported by the Inspection and Acceptance Report under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were still not implemented due to manpower and time constraints.
observations.reason: The recommendations were still not implemented due to manpower and time constraints.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following measures:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and submit the complete RPCPPE for all types of property of the LGU;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and reconcile their property records; and
c. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish verifiable PPE balances as to existence, condition and accountability.

3.12. We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare and submit supporting detail/ schedules, aging schedules and provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
b. Conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other funds and Due to Other Fund accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Reclassify the current and non-current portion of its outstanding loans payable account to present fairly in the financial statements; and
b) Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary loan details such as, the terms and conditions of the loan which includes the interest rate, maturity date and quarterly/semi-annual amortization to enhance the understandability of the users of financial statements.

observations.managementAction: Manpower constraints prevented full compliance; partial actions taken included utilization advisories and some monitoring steps.
observations.reason: Manpower constraints and other constraints prevented full implementation of certain monitoring and documentation-related recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure that a copy of the Memoranda of Agreements is obtained and submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from perfection thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 for proper review and to facilitate evaluation and monitoring of the projects/services completed out of the funds transferred to the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) to require all job orders, casual and contractual employees to prepare their Daily Time Records and submit Accomplishment Report signed and verified by the immediate supervisors before processing claims for payment in compliance with Item 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a) Require the LDRRMO together with the members of the LDRRMC to identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and effect the full utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the LGU’s disaster preparedness;
b) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain a separate registry for the appropriations, allotments, and obligations of local disaster risk reduction and management fund specifically on 30 percent (30%) QRF, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay; and
c) Require the Management to create the mandatory positions as mandated under Section 12.b of RA 10121 to ensure the implementation of programmed activities and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRMM council overlooked the inclusion of projects to be funded from the previous years' unexpended QRF.
Some actions taken: maintenance of registry and creation of mandatory positions undertaken.
observations.reason: The LDRMM council overlooked the inclusion of projects to be funded from the previous years' unexpended QRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers and their accompanying supporting documents after payment has been made to prevent the possible re-use of the same on similar transactions, thus, safeguarding government funds against unnecessary loss through double settlement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from processing and paying travel claims without the required supporting documents.
Further, require the submission of the lacking documentary requirement for travel listed in Annex E, for the Audit Team not to issue a notice of suspension.

We recommended:
• Exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and enforce strict compliance with the regulations provided in RA 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 on the grant of honoraria to officers/employees involved in government procurement to prevent excessive and unlawful payments;
• Limit the payment of honoraria to the source identified in the said Budget Circular; and
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that savings from the approved local budget were duly approved by the Sanggunian before effecting any payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat chargeable to the General Fund and to further treat collections from the sale of bid documents as receipts in the Trust Fund (TF) books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to meticulously and judiciously prepare the Annual Procurement Plan based on the PPMPs submitted by the different departments/offices in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and ensure that all necessary information required in the APP and its amendments, if any, are reflected therein to serve as planning and control tool for efficient procurement.

1. Hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports; and  

2. Direct both the Municipal Accountant & Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the reports, however, the perennial issue of late submission still persists.
observations.reason: Due to time and manpower constraints, Management was not able to submit the reports with the deadlines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit and prepare the Report of Check Issued to the Municipal Accountant in compliance to  Section 43 Volume I and Section 55 Volume II of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).
observations.managementAction: RCIs were actualluy prepared but not submitted to support the DVs.
observations.reason: Per verification, the RCIs were prepared however, these were not submitted to cover the DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to:

Require the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee, through the BAC Secretariat, to post the notices of award and the notices to proceed for all the awarded projects in the PhilGeps and the website of the procuring entity, respectively; and

Require the Bids and Awards Committee members to attend seminars and/or trainings on the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR to be able to adhere, in all stages of the procurement of goods and services and infrastructure projects to achieve transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management is still on the process of complying with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Management still failed to implement the recommendations mainly because of time and manpower constraints.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Fill up the vacant MPDC positions and accordingly provide needed appropriation for a more effective administration and delivery of the basic  services needed by the constituents; and

Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of position.
observations.managementAction: Management actualy initiated the selection process of applicants. The selected applicant lacks the newly imposed requirement of elegibility for the position.
observations.reason: Management could have already filled up the position  but due to the new qualification or eligibility required for the position, this has not been implemented during CY2021.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain three separate registries of LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a list or report on expenditures related to COVID-19; and
Require the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Management the following:

a.)	Refrain from granting cash advances without a specific legal purpose and to also supply a clear-cut description as to the purpose of the drawing of cash advance to preclude the issuance of the audit suspensions/disallowances, and
b.)	Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Disbursement of each cash advance together with liquidation reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GSO and Accountant to maintain property cards and PPE ledger cards, and instigate the conduct of physical inventory of the agency’s PPE. The inventory listing of the equipment should be compared with the property card maintained by the Municipal General Services Office as against the equipment ledger cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the total thereof should be reconciled with the control accounts in the general ledger. All discrepancies noted between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

a.) Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count over the LGU’s Local Road Network which should be done at least once a year; and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Road Network System and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
b.) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
c.) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
d.) Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments for the reclassification of the expenses that were inappropriately recorded in the Other Financial Charges account for the fair presentation of account balance in the financial statements; and for future transactions, to appropriately classify the expenses and properly accomplish the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs). Henceforth, the Accounting Office should always observe the proper classification and recording of accounts to avoid misrepresentation of accounts and abuse of accounts for balancing.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to refrain from charging prior years’ expenses against the current year’s appropriation. This shall be initiated by the incurrence of expenses according to available appropriation. If the incurrence of expenses during year-ends cannot be dispensed with in the exigency of service, unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years. For the process to be valid however, said payables shall be duly supported with complete documentations.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the officials/employees concerned and GSO-Designate to fill-out the relevant and required information on the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders and such documents must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete and ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

Recommendations:
We  have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Require the Human Resource Management Officer to hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports; and
b.) Direct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the official concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed period for the submission of paid disbursement vouchers and other financial reports: (1) the Municipal Treasurer— within the first five (5) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate prompt recording of transactions in the books of accounts, and (2) the Municipal Accountant—within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Audit Team. When DV lacks supporting documents, return the same to the concerned Office for completion.

Close coordination between the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other Department Head to facilitate timely submission of the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents.

Request from the Local Chief Executive additional personnel in the Accounting Office to augment the workforce to facilitate the timely recording and preparation of financial reports.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit and prepare the Report of Check Issued to the Municipal Accountant in compliance with Section 43 Volume I and Section 55 Volume II of the New Government Accounting System Manual (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

Recommendation:
We have recommended that all deliveries of goods, equipment, services and completed/accomplished infrastructure projects shall be passed thru with an inspection by the Agency Inspection Committee or the Agency Inspector and the COA representative, duly accepted and approved by the Agency Head. No claims of Suppliers/Contractor shall be paid unless supported by the Inspection and Acceptance Report under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC), or other officer assigned, as the case may be, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the HRM Officer, to:

a.) Make all the necessary actions to be able to select and appoint a MPDC who meets the qualifications mandated under Section 476 so as to accordingly provide needed appropriation for a more effective administration and delivery of the basic services needed by the constituents; and
b.)	Strictly observe the rules/requirements on Human Resource Action particularly the designation procedure as provided in the Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer to reconcile both of their records, the subsidiary ledgers and stock cards respectively.  If the GSO does not have stock cards, require her to maintain one;

Require the Designated General Services Officer with the inventory committee to complete the conduct a regular physical count on Property, Plant and Equipment once a year with the required observers such as any representative of COA and correspondingly submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team;

Likewise, require the inventory committee to observe the appropriate procedures on property management such as:

•	Assign property numbers to all PPE
•	ARE/ MR/ PAR should be renewed every three years
•	Physical Inventory Sticker shall be used
•	Infrastructure Assets should be included in the physical count

To reconcile accounts between the Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the contracts, however, the perennial issue of late submission still persists.
observations.reason: The recommendations by the Audit Team  werestill not implemented due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the General Service Officer to design and pin appropriate label or tag to each unit of properties and equipment of the city government in compliance with Section 123 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 80-124 (Part V, part 2) in order to facilitate the conduct of property inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal General Services Officer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant & Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The municipality in itself is already understaffed and Heads of Offices also perform other tasks as well as the staffs of different departments.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Designated General Services Officer to reconcile both of their records, the subsidiary ledgers and stock cards respectively.  If the GSO does not have stock cards, require her to maintain one;
Require the Designated General Services Officer with the inventory committee to complete the conduct a regular physical count on Property, Plant and Equipment once a year with the required observers such as any representative of COA and correspondingly submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team;
Likewise, require the inventory committee to observe the appropriate procedures on property management such as:
Assign property numbers to all PPE
ARE/ MR/ PAR should be renewed every three years
Physical Inventory Sticker shall be used
Infrastructure Assets should be included in the physical count
To reconcile accounts between the Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports, post the same in the appropriate website, bulletin board or conspicuous places, and to submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to the audit team.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports; and
Direct both the Municipal Accountant & Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to inform all department heads and office personnel to strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 relative to the required set of documentary requirements on the liquidation/reimbursement of travel claims, and to further require the employees to properly accomplish their Itinerary of Travel.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit and prepare the Report of Check Issued to the Municipal Accountant in compliance to  Section 43 Volume I and Section 55 Volume II of the New Government Accounting System Manual (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Disbursement of each cash advance together with the Liquidation Disbursement Vouchers in compliance of Section 43 Volume I and Section 55 Volume II of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs).
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer attach to the pertinent DVs the duplicate copies of all checks issued.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee, through the BAC Secretariat, to post the notices of award and the notices to proceed for all the awarded projects in the PhilGeps and the website of the procuring entity, respectively; and
Require the Bids and Awards Committee members to attend seminars and/or trainings on the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR to be able to adhere, in all stages of the procurement of goods and services and infrastructure projects to achieve transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the General Service Officer to design and pin appropriate label or tag to each unit of properties and equipment of the city government in compliance with Section 123 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 80-124 (Part V, part 2) in order to facilitate the conduct of property inventory taking.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Fill up the vacant MPDC positions and accordingly provide needed appropriation for a more effective administration and delivery of the basic  services needed by the constituents; and
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of position.

The Accountable Officer/Collector should remit her collections to the Municipal Treasurer and deposit the same to its depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and R.A. 7160 and to strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year; and 

The Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officer/Collector regularly remits her collections to the Municipal Treasurer and deposit the same to its depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and R.A. 7160 and had strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year;

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using cash advance to pay the operating expenses of the Municipality and to ensure that disbursements be issued with check directly to the name of the payees, concerned employees, and suppliers.
observations.managementAction: This was partially implemented due to the pandemic of Covid-19, suppliers were reluctant to offer or transact with the LGU on an account basis especially now that movements are limited.

observations.recommendation: To require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to follow the remittance schedule for the Government contributions and Employee’s contribution as well as loan amortization or payments to the Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS fund pursuant to pertinent provisions of the IRRs of RA No. 9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275 so that the Municipality could avoid the payment of penalty and surcharges for late remittance of premiums.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant followed the remittance schedule for the Government contributions and Employee’s contribution as well as loan amortization or payments to the Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS fund pursuant to pertinent provisions of the IRRs of RA No. 9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275 so that the Municipality could avoid the payment of penalty and surcharges for late remittance of premiums.
The cashbooks maintained by the Municipal Treasurer were already regularly updated in accordance to the provisions of Section 29 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers to support the General Ledger.

Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with Section 111 of PD 1445 and Section 12 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II, on the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers with the balance in the General Ledger to ensure the accuracy of the account.
observations.managementAction: The management now strictly comply with Section 111 of PD 1445 and Section 12 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II, on the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers with the balance in the General Ledger to ensure the accuracy of the account.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency. 

Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: It was not fully implemented due to the Pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations: 

The Accountable Officers/ Collectors should remit/ turnover their collections to the Municipal Treasurer and deposit the same to its depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and R.A. 7160 and to strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year. 

The Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer admitted that they don’t make deposits for every ₱5,000 collected and that due to her trust in her collectors, she would sometimes fail to monitor if deposits were duly made. She explained that the collectors were bonded and were allowed or designated to make the deposits of the collections to the bank. However, she said that she will deposit the collections until their trusted Revenue Collector report back on duty and will have the Mayor issue an Office Order to appoint their trusted Revenue Collector as the Liquidating Officer to make the deposits of the collections to the bank. The Municipal Accountant said that they are currently processing the transfer of the various account of the LGU from LBP-Tubod to LBP-Kapatagan.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Request the Procuring Officer to properly accomplish the Purchase Orders indicating all the vital information required as provided in Section B of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 5.2 of COA Circular 97-004 to promote effective and efficient operations of the Municipality.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop processing procurement of supplies, materials and equipment with incomplete data and information in the forms of Purchase Order.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that due to lack of personnel, the management has no Procuring Officer, and that since the BAC Chairman is new, the BAC Secretariat also functions as the GSO and at the same time the Procuring Officer. She stated that she will inform the Mayor the recommendations above as well as to suggest appointing new personnel to be the new GSO/Procuring Officer.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

Required the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly the provisions of IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the advertisement of Invitation to Bid so that all procurement activities will result in the most advantageous price/terms for the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management said that they adhere and practice PhilGEPS. The BAC Chairman said that she will consult the BAC Secretariat and will furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader proofs that they have posted the mentioned projects in the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Accountable Officer/Collector to remitt her collections and deposit the same to its depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and R.A. 7160 and to strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year; and
She should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using cash advance to pay the operating expenses of the Municipality and to ensure that disbursements be issued with check directly to the name of the payees, concerned employees, and suppliers.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
Require both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to follow the remittance schedule for the Government contributions and Employee’s contribution as well as loan amortization or payments to the Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS fund pursuant to pertinent provisions of the IRRs of RA No. 9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275 so that the Municipality could avoid the payment of penalty and surcharges for late remittance of premiums.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal General Service s Officer and create a special team to design and pin appropriate label or tag to each unit of properties and equipment of the municipal government in compliance with Section 123 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 80-124 (Part V, part 2) in order to facilitate the conduct of property inventory taking.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct both the Municipal Accountant & Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers to support the General Ledger.
Henceforth, Management should strictly comply with Section 111 of PD 1445 and Section 12 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II, on the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers with the balance in the General Ledger to ensure the accuracy of the account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency.
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We have recommended the following:

Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports.

Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the cash advances accounts, advised the concerned officials/employees to liquidate their advances and if possible continue to apply thru payroll deductions.

Advised to stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those with unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, appropriate disciplinary measures shall be imposed against then should they continue to violate Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer explained that the liquidations of above listed cash advances were already submitted. Regarding the cash advance for the Barangay –DILG, the Barangays deposited their payment for the registration to the Municipal Treasury and required the Municipal Treasurer to cash advance. The Municipal Accountant explained that the registration for the said Seminar was made through the LGU so that the barangays will be issued an official receipt for them to attach for their liquidations. She further explained that the submissions of the liquidations were delayed because of the delays of the attachments and signatories. However, she said that they would do their best to comply with the recommendations and minimize the delays of submission.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Required the Accountant to submit the required documents as enumerated in Section 5.2 of COA Circular 2012-001:

Certificate of evidence of services rendered or actual work/attendance of the official;

Certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle; and

Payroll Register
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that this is the first time that she had encountered the said AOM and that she will check the requirements stated in the COA Circular. She further stated that the management doesn’t have any government vehicle to use or to assign to. Thus, will inform the Sanguniang Bayan regarding the recommendation and will attach the requirements needed in claiming the RATA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Municipal Vice Mayor to apply for a fidelity bond, approved the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the sanggunian only and shall be allowed to countersign in the check drawn.

Issue resolution authorizing the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Municipal treasurer as the recognize signatory of their check disbursement for the Office of the Vice Mayor and Sanggunian Bayan.

The Municipal Budget Officer to compute the monthly appropriation of the Vice Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan Members to be transferred to the Account of the general fund – vice mayor appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed that the Municipal Vice Mayor will be applied a bond  year since budgets are already appropriated for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal General Services Officer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the delays are due to the delay of acquisition of the attachments and signatories needed, that this is a “domino effect”, if there is a delay in the Municipal Treasury, it would follow that there would also be a delay in the Accounting Office. She stated that the management still doesn’t have a GSO designate but will recommend to the Mayor to appoint one. She further explained that before, they have problems obtaining bank statements and that due to having an unstable internet connection, even though they have enrolled in I-Access, they still encounter problems obtaining bank statements. However, she is confident that the Mayor is mindful of the AOMs, due to the SGLG who keeps updating the criteria, and that they will do their best to adhere with the recommendation and hopefully submit report, especially the bank reconciliation, within the prescribed timetable.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned LGU officials to prepare and submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer admitted that they have submitted a copy of the approved GAD Plan and Budget as well as the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the DILG but not to the Office of the Audit Team Leader. The Municipal Accountant said that they already even have a GPB for CY 2020 and said that they will furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader a copy of said plans along with the Accomplishment Reports.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports.
Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the cash advances accounts, advised the concerned officials/employees to liquidate their advances and if possible continue to apply thru payroll deductions.
Advised to stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those with unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, appropriate disciplinary measures shall be imposed against then should they continue to violate Section 89 of PD 1445.

The Accountable Officers/Collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the Municipal Treasurer and deposit the same to its depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 and 32 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and R.A. 7160 and to strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year.
The Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.

Required the Accountant to submit the required documents as enumerated in Section 5.2 of COA Circular 2012-001:
Certificate of evidence of services rendered or actual work/attendance of the official;
Certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle; and
Payroll Register

The Municipal Vice Mayor to apply for a fidelity bond, approved the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the sanggunian only and shall be allowed to countersigned in the check drawn.
Issue resolution authorizing the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Municipal treasurer as the recognize signatory of their check disbursement for the Office of the Vice Mayor and Sanggunian Bayan.
The Municipal Budget Officer to compute the monthly appropriation of the Vice Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan Members to be transferred to the Account of the general fund – vice mayor appropriation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal General Services Officer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Request the Procuring Officer to properly accomplish the Purchase Orders indicating all the vital information required as provided in Section B of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 5.2 of COA Circular 97-004 to promote effective and efficient operations of the Municipality.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop processing procurement of supplies, materials and equipment with incomplete data and information in the forms of Purchase Order.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned LGU officials to prepare and submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly the provisions of IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the advertisement of Invitation to Bid so that all procurement activities will result in the most advantageous price/terms for the LGU.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the LDRRMO officer and other concerned employees to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, Separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and the Annual DRRM Plan, Budget and Programs to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

Strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 relative to the required set of documentary requirements on the liquidation/reimbursement of travel claims, and to further require the employees to properly accomplish their Itinerary of Travel.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned departments to set commitment schedule showing the name of reports and the deadline of submission to be posted in any conspicuous place within their respective offices to serve as continuing reminders of compliance.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to comply with the requirement on notification and publicity of PPAs so that the public may be regularly informed of the projects being implemented by the Municipality; and 
Likewise, implementing departments/offices/units submit the monthly monitoring reports for projects for validation by the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Engineer to include in the submission of Bidding Documents, the Approved Budget for the Contract as mandated under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 for review to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

To instruct the BAC Secretariat to regularly furnish copies of contract of infrastructure projects to enable the local auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial reviews as well as future reference of COA-TAS in the conduct of technical review and inspection in pursuant to the guidelines on Manual on Contracts Review.

Instruct the Bids and Award Committee, BAC-Secretariat and BAC –twg to ensure the timely preparation and submission of contracts and Purchase Orders and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with project and make the necessary request for inspection of COA engineer upon completion of projects prior to its final payment.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Give priority in implementing projects under the 20% development fund to attain the project objectives of the programs/projects/activities of the Annual Investment Program so as to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for the general public in accordance with Section 287 of the Local Government Code.

The Local Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Officer should closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs/activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with Par. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012;
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

That the 10 year plan of the Solid Waste Management of the Municipality shall be properly reviewed and updated in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Council as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
That a Sanitary landfill shall be established and the controlled dumpsite shall be closed.
To ensure efficiency and effectiveness of the program, we also recommended the following:

Established a Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or Associations.
Discouraged the use of cellophanes by all business owners/operators and replaced it with paper bags.
Both the residents and the owners of commercial establishments should segregate the waste as to biodegradable and non-biodegradable before pick up by the garbage bags.
Receptacles should be properly labeled as to biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes.
 Imposed strict compliance as to collection of biodegradable and non-biodegradable rather.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Head of the Human Resource Officer to furnish a copy of the Accumulated Leave Credits in the Master List Personnel in the LGU to the Accounting Office.
Direct the Budget Officer to appropriate every year the appropriation for the accrued payable on terminal leave benefits earned by each employee even on a staggered basis depending on the availability of funds.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to  make the necessary adjustments to  record the accrued leave payable after ensuring the  correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances of employees as of December 31, 2017.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to set up a special fund for the purpose.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned departments to set commitment schedule showing the name of reports and the deadline of submission to be posted in any conspicuous place within their respective offices to serve as continuing reminders of compliance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to comply with the requirement on notification and publicity of PPAs so that the public may be regularly informed of the projects being implemented by the Municipality; and
Likewise, implementing departments/offices/units submit the monthly monitoring reports for projects for validation by the Auditor.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Engineer to include in the submission of Bidding Documents, the Approved Budget for the Contract as mandated under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 for review to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
To instruct the BAC Secretariat to regularly furnish copies of contract of infrastructure projects to enable the local auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial reviews as well as future reference of COA-TAS in the conduct of technical review and inspection in pursuant to the guidelines on Manual on Contracts Review.
Instruct the Bids and Award Committee, BAC-Secretariat and BAC –twg to ensure the timely preparation and submission of contracts and Purchase Orders and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with project and make the necessary request for inspection of COA engineer upon completion of projects prior to its final payment.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with Par. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012;

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Give priority in implementing projects under the 20% development fund to attain the project objectives of the programs/projects/activities of the Annual Investment Program so as to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for the general public in accordance with Section 287 of the Local Government Code.
The Local Development Council and Municipal Planning and Development Officer should closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs/activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
That the 10 year plan of the Solid Waste Management of the Municipality shall be properly reviewed and updated in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Council as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
That a Sanitary landfill shall be established and the controlled dumpsite shall be closed.
To ensure efficiency and effectiveness of the program, we also recommended the following:
Established a Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or Associations.
Discouraged the use of cellophanes by all business owners/operators and replaced it with paper bags.
Both the residents and the owners of commercial establishments should segregate the waste as to biodegradable and non-biodegradable before pick up by the garbage bags.
Receptacles should be properly labeled as to biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes.
Imposed strict compliance as to collection of biodegradable and non-biodegradable rather.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial reports on time in pursuant to Section 122 of P.D 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Management of the LGU Sapad informed the Audit team that in as much as they would comply with the requirement on the timely submission of the Reports, they are delayed in the submission, as noted , because the vouchers coming from the Treasurer’s Office were forwarded to them late due to the Municipal Treasurer’s death which has greatly affected the processing of the vouchers. However, the Management assured that they will exert effort to comply with the recommendations indicated in the findings.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

a. In the absence of the General Services Officer, the Municipal Treasurer      should processed and secure copy of Transfer Certificate of Title of the 9 lots for absolute ownership purposes based on the Tax Declaration.

b. Include the recording of land in the Report of Physical Count of Property,    Plant and Equipment.
c. The Local Chief Executive should designate the Municipal General Services Officer in order not to hamper the work of the Municipal Treasurer and instead focus on the custody and accountability of all properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, Management commented that they were not able to implement in 2016 because of the LGU’s lack of GSO to facilitate the recording and inventory of PPE accounts. To comply with the recommendations, the Management will designate a GSO so they can facilitate the inventory of the PPA   as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

The concerned procuring Officer thru the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat should submit copies of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with to enable the assigned auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial review as well as future reference to COA-Technical Services Office in the conduct of technical review and inspection.
observations.managementAction: In response to the foregoing audit observation, Management commented during the exit conference that due the LGU’s lack of manpower and GSO, the submission of the perfected contracts and its supporting documents was not submitted on time. However, to comply with the recommendations, the Management promised to designate a GSO so they can facilitate the submission of the contracts and its supporting as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Local School Board should consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable against the Special Education Fund pursuant to the provision of RA 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and implementing of DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, as amended during the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year.
Stop the charges and payment of expenditures for the operation of the district office and schools which are not directly attributable to the establishment and operation of extension classes
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, Management affirmed the deficiencies noted and further reasoned that they lack extensive knowledge and are not well-versed with respect to the rules on Special Education Fund (SEF) utilization due to lack of seminars and orientation. Nonetheless, the Management further committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer-designate and Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Require the LDRRM officer to incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.
Require the Local Disaster Risk Management officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports under COA Circular NO. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, Management reasoned out that they already request an SB resolution as required by the LBP in opening another LGU account for the purpose for special trust fund for the LDRRMF. Nonetheless, the Management further committed to comply with the audit recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the copies of the contracts for the projects contracted as shown herewith as (Annex I) and all the supporting documents which formed part thereof be submitted to our office as soon as possible.

Moreover, we recommend that just in case there would be new projects to be contracted in the future, copies of their contracts with the supporting documents should be submitted to our office within five (5) working days after perfection and/or approval thereof.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Contracts for Infrastructure Project were not submitted to the office of the Auditor within five (5) working days after the perfection and/or approval thereof by the Municipal Treasurer since he was then handling multiple jobs. He committed to submit within the prescribed period all perfected contracts of following year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial reports on time in pursuant to Section 122 of P.D 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

a. In the absence of the General Services Officer, the Municipal Treasurer      should processed and secure copy of Transfer Certificate of Title of the 9 lots for absolute ownership purposes based on the Tax Declaration.
b. Include the recording of land in the Report of Physical Count of Property,    Plant and Equipment.
c. The Local Chief Executive should designate the Municipal General Services Officer in order not to hamper the work of the Municipal Treasurer and instead focus on the custody and accountability of all properties of the Municipality.

The concerned procuring Officer thru the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat should submit copies of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with to enable the assigned auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial review as well as future reference to COA-Technical Services Office in the conduct of technical review and inspection.

The Local School Board should consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable against the Special Education Fund pursuant to the provision of RA 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and implementing of DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, as amended during the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year.
Stop the charges and payment of expenditures for the operation of the district office and schools which are not directly attributable to the establishment and operation of extension classes.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer-designate and Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Require the LDRRM officer to incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.
Require the Local Disaster Risk Management officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports under COA Circular NO. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format .

Members of the GFPS, as spearheaded by the MSWDO, conduct examination of the agency’s level of gender mainstreaming or extent of the gender responsiveness of the Municipal programs and activities, the level of gender awareness and competence of its personnel and the presence or absence of enabling mechanisms that support gender mainstreaming.
Based on the results of gender audit, the GFPS determine programs, projects and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.
The TWG prepare the consolidated GAD accomplishment Report (AR) based from the DILG approved GPB following the instructions and format prescribed under the aforementioned Joint Circular to provide consistent data for more accurate assessment of actual accomplishments.

We recommend that the Accounting Office should strictly observe the prescribed time of submission of bank reconciliation statements and strict adherence to prescribed rules and regulations provided in Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant  admitted that she was then handling multiple jobs that’s why she was not able to prepare and submit on time the required Bank Reconciliation Statement. She promised to work it out to adhere  the provisions of the above mentioned laws.
